arts.ttl: Starting XI: Is this the Sunderland team to face West Ham on our Premier League return?
arts.sum: Well, here it is lads and lasses – it’s all going to get very real, very quickly! We’ve obviously had an influx of new players coming into the club, which means the starting XI will be almost unrecognisable from that which was victorious against Sheffield United at Wembley 12 weeks or so ago. The pre-season […]

arts.ttl: Sunderland and West Ham both have reasons to dread this opening fixture
arts.sum: Long awaited, the Premier League returns with West Ham fans the less enthusiastic about the forthcoming season: Two players brought in and two on ‘free’ transfers hardly qualifies as a ‘rebuild’. Something all Hammers fans will be aware of is the clubs dreadful opening-day form in the Premier League. Indeed, West Ham have come out […] Source

arts.ttl: "We have to accept that we will suffer" - Regis Le Bris previews the upcoming season ahead of West Ham clash
arts.sum: The Frenchman also added that this provided an opportunity for Dan Neil, Sunderland's captain last season, to develop. For us as a club, a squad, as a coach and as a manager, it's a chance with this new experience to grow and that is the same for Dan."

arts.ttl: Sunderland holding off letting player leave on loan – May need him for West Ham
arts.sum: NEC Nijmegen are continuing to work on a deal for Sunderland defender Jenson Seelt but may need to wait until later in the transfer window to get him. That’s according to ‘club watcher’ for Voetbal International, Sander Janssen’, via Voetbal Zone, who reports hat Sunderland still have short-term plans with Seelt at this moment in The post Sunderland holding off letting player leave on loan – May need him for West Ham appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Forget Fernandes, West Ham’s best news goes under the radar ahead of Sunderland clash
arts.sum: With the transfer window now heading towards the last two weeks – thank heavens – West Ham fans have been largely preoccupied with the club’s mixed dealings in the market place and the saga that is the attempt to upgrade the midfield. Mateus Fernandes has been filling the headlines for the last week as the […] Source
